skill.name: accessibility-check
skill.description: Review UI pages and components for WCAG 2.1 accessibility issues, including keyboard access, labels, contrast, focus states, and screen reader behavior.
skill.summary: Audit UI accessibility against WCAG 2.1 AA with practical fixes.
skill.author: Dami-Randle

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer reported many high and medium alerts, but most are false positives caused by WCAG text, markdown code fences, and accessibility examples. The confirmed risk is that the skill recommends running npx Playwright and pa11y commands, including a localhost URL, which can execute local tooling and perform a local HTTP scan. No prompt injection, credential access, filesystem modification, or external exfiltration was found in SKILL.md.

medium_findings.title: User-Run External Accessibility Commands
medium_findings.description: The skill documents npx Playwright and pa11y commands for accessibility testing. These commands are legitimate for this skill, but they can execute local Node tooling or downloaded packages if the user runs them in an untrusted project.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are clearly present in a bash example and match the skill purpose. Risk is limited because they are documentation-only and require explicit user execution.

low_findings.title: Localhost URL in Testing Example
low_findings.description: The pa11y example targets http://localhost:5176. This is a local accessibility scan example, not evidence of data exfiltration or contact with an external service.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL is limited to localhost and appears inside an accessibility testing section. No remote host, secret handling, or upload behavior is present.

low_findings.title: Static Pattern False Positives in Markdown Content
low_findings.description: Several static alerts for weak cryptography, reconnaissance, and shell backticks are false positives. The cited lines contain WCAG checklist text, markdown fences, JSX or CSS examples, and accessibility guidance rather than executable security-sensitive code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review shows these lines are prose, markdown templates, or static examples. They do not contain runtime code paths, cryptographic use, or reconnaissance commands.

dangerous_patterns.title: npx Command Execution Example
dangerous_patterns.description: The documented commands use npx for Playwright and pa11y. Users should run them only in trusted projects with reviewed dependencies.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern is visible in the markdown command block. It is a valid testing workflow, but npx can execute package code.

content.value_statement: Accessibility defects block users from completing important tasks and can create compliance risk. This skill guides WCAG 2.1 checks with clear findings, test notes, and fixes.
actual_capabilities: Reviews UI pages and components against WCAG 2.1 A and AA criteria.
Checks keyboard access, focus visibility, page titles, headings, labels, and ARIA usage.
Guides automated accessibility testing with Playwright and pa11y examples.
Structures results into compliance summaries, issue tables, testing notes, and recommendations.
Suggests common fixes for alt text, form labels, contrast, and focus indicators.
limitations: Requires user-supplied UI context, source code, screenshots, or a running page.
Does not run tests automatically unless the user requests execution and tools are available.
Does not replace legal certification or research with disabled users.
Focuses on WCAG 2.1, so WCAG 2.2 criteria may need extra review.

use_cases.title: Pre-Release UI Audit
use_cases.description: Check a feature page for WCAG 2.1 AA issues before release and receive prioritized fixes.

use_cases.title: Regression Review
use_cases.description: Review keyboard navigation, focus order, labels, and contrast after a UI change.

use_cases.title: Design System Review
use_cases.description: Evaluate shared components for accessible defaults before teams reuse them across products.

prompt_templates.title: Quick Accessibility Check
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of one page or component.
prompt_templates.prompt: Check this page or component for WCAG 2.1 AA accessibility issues. Summarize critical problems, warnings, and practical fixes.

prompt_templates.title: Keyboard Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit keyboard access for this interface. Check tab order, focus visibility, activation keys, escape behavior, and keyboard traps.

prompt_templates.title: Screen Reader Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this UI for screen reader support. Check headings, landmarks, labels, names, roles, values, and meaningful reading order.

prompt_templates.title: Release Accessibility Audit
prompt_templates.prompt: Perform a WCAG 2.1 AA release audit. Include pass status, issue priority, affected criteria, testing notes, and remediation steps.

output_examples.input: Audit a signup form for WCAG 2.1 AA compliance.
output: Critical issue: Email input needs a programmatic label.
Warning: Error text should be linked to the field.
Recommended next step: Add explicit labels and verify screen reader announcements.

output_examples.input: Check a modal dialog for keyboard accessibility.
output: Critical issue: Focus can move behind the dialog.
Warning: Escape behavior is not documented.
Recommended next step: Trap focus inside the dialog and return focus to the opener.

output_examples.input: Review a button style for contrast and focus visibility.
output: Critical issue: Disabled and secondary button text may not meet contrast requirements.
Warning: Focus outline is visible but low contrast.
Recommended next step: Increase contrast and test at 200 percent zoom.

best_practices: Provide the page URL, component source, screenshots, and target WCAG level.
Combine automated checks with manual keyboard and screen reader review.
Verify fixes in the final UI, not only in isolated code snippets.
anti_patterns: Treating color contrast checks as a complete accessibility audit.
Adding ARIA before using semantic HTML where native elements work.
Ignoring keyboard behavior in custom menus, dialogs, and controls.

faq.question: What accessibility standard does this skill use?
faq.answer: It focuses on WCAG 2.1, especially Level A and Level AA checks.

faq.question: Can it run automated accessibility tests?
faq.answer: It can guide Playwright and pa11y testing when the environment supports those tools.

faq.question: Does it replace a legal accessibility audit?
faq.answer: No. It supports technical review, but it is not a legal certification service.

faq.question: Can it review screenshots only?
faq.answer: It can identify visual issues from screenshots, but source code or a running page improves accuracy.

faq.question: Does it cover screen reader behavior?
faq.answer: Yes. It checks semantic structure, labels, roles, values, and expected announcements.

faq.question: Who should use this skill?
faq.answer: Frontend developers, QA engineers, designers, and design system teams can use it before release.
